PERSONAL INJURY/PROPERTY DAMAGE - VEHICLE RELATED, COMPLAINT FILED BY PLAINTIFF SIMPSON, ALLEN R AS TO DEFENDANT BANK OF AMERICA NT & SA(S) SUMMONS ISSUED, JUDICIAL COUNCIL CIVIL CASE COVER SHEET FILED CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE SCHEDULED FOR MAY-22-2009 PROOF OF SERVICE DUE ON                  FEB-20-2009 CASE MANAGEMENT STATEMENT DUE ON         MAY-07-2009
ORDER EXTENDING TIME TO SERVE TO AUGUST 28, 2009; EX PARTE APPLICATION FOR EXTENSION OF TIME TO SERVE PLEADING

LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANT BANK OF AMERICA, N.A. DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT. PRO PER PLAINTIFF FAILED TO GIVE NOTICE, PURSUANT TO SAN FRANCISCO LOCAL RULE 8.3. THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING AS FOLLOWS: DEMURRER SUSTAINED WITHOUT LEAVE TO AMEND. JUDGMENT WAS ENTERED IN FAVOR OF DEFENDANT AND AGAINST PLAINTIFF IN CASE NUMBER CGC-01-420367. THIS ACTION IS BARRED BY THE PRINCIPLE OF RES JUDICATA. NO REASONABLE PROBABILITY THAT DEFECT CAN BE CURED BY AMENDMENT. (PREVAILING PARTY TO PREPARE A FORM OF ORDER.) JUDGE: CHARLOTTE WALTER WOOLARD, REPORTER: KENT GUBBINE, CSR #5797

DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Dec-01-2009 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: DEFENDANT BANK OF AMERICA, N.A. DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT. Appearances: Allen Simpson, Plaintiff in Pro Per appearing. Pro Per Plaintiff failed to give notice, pursuant to San Francisco Local Rule 8.3. The Court adopted its tentative ruling as follows: Demurrer sustained without leave to amend. Judgment was entered in favor of defendant and against plaintiff in case number CGC-01-420367. This action is barred by the principle of res judicata. No reasonable probability that defect can be cured by amendment. (Prevailing party to prepare a form of order.) (302/CWW)Judge: Charlotte Walter Woolard; Court Reporter: Kent Gubbine CSR 5797
